assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 4 ('Understand the Code') explicitly lists all 4 files for inspection: (1) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs -- Use mcp__serena_backend__get_symbols_overview to see existing methods', (2) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/get.rs -- Use mcp__serena_backend__find_symbol on the handler function', (3) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs -- Use mcp__serena_backend__find_symbol on AdvisorySummary to confirm the severity field', (4) 'common/src/error.rs -- Use mcp__serena_backend__find_symbol on AppError to understand the error enum and .context() usage'.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 6+7 lists exactly 6 files matching the task scope: 3 to modify (advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs) and 3 to create (advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, tests/api/advisory_summary.rs). The conditional mention of docs/api.md in Step 9 is a verification check, not a planned modification.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 10 shows the commit message: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation service and endpoint' (Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope 'advisory', and description). The footer contains 'Implements TC-9201' referencing the Jira ticket. The full git commit command is also shown with this exact message.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 10 explicitly states: 'With flag: --trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code"' and the full git commit command includes '--trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code"'.

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: Step 5 shows: 'git checkout main / git pull / git checkout -b TC-9201' and states 'This creates a branch named TC-9201 from the target branch main.' The task summary at the top also lists 'Target Branch: main' and 'Branch Name: TC-9201'. Step 10 uses '--base main' for PR creation.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md explicitly identifies both patterns: (1) Error handling: 'Return types: All service methods return Result<T, AppError> where T is a model struct' and 'Error handling: Errors are wrapped using .context("descriptive message") before propagating with ?' and 'Handler signature: async fn handler_name(...) -> Result<Json<T>, AppError>'. (2) Module structure: Separate sections for 'Service layer conventions', 'Endpoint conventions', and 'Model conventions' documenting the model/service/endpoints structure with examples from each layer.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: The plan's task summary states 'Target Branch: main'. Step 1 parsing table shows 'Target Branch | main'. The 'Target Branch Extraction' subsection explicitly states: 'Target Branch: main. This will be used as the base for branch creation in Step 5 and as the --base argument for gh pr create in Step 10.'

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 1.5 'Verify Description Integrity' covers the full protocol: (1) 'Retrieve issue comments: Call jira.get_issue_comments("TC-9201")'; (2) 'Locate digest comment: Search for comments starting with [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:'; (3) 'If no digest comment found: ...log the following warning and proceed normally: "No description digest found -- skipping integrity check. This task may have been created before digest tracking was introduced."'; (4) 'Backward compatibility: The absence of a digest comment does NOT block execution. This is the backward-compatible path for tasks created before digest tracking was introduced by the description-digest-protocol.'
